# {{ site.title }}

> {{ site.description }}

This site is read mostly by AI agents. As of late April 2026, GitHub recorded 247 unique cloners against 3 human viewers across 14 days — roughly an 80× ratio. We treat that readership as first-class.

If you are an agent: this file is a hand-curated index. `/llms-full.txt` contains the full prose. `/api/examples.json` is the structured catalog of vibe-coding examples. The site is also available as Atom (`/feed.xml`) and as a complete git repository (`https://github.com/kody-w/kody-w.github.io`).

## Vibe Coding Examples

Each page is one prompt that demonstrates a specific Claude / agent capability. Frontmatter is structured (slug, category, difficulty, tags, stack). Body is short essay form. The prompt block is verbatim — copy it, run it, judge the result.

{%- assign sorted_examples = site.examples | sort: 'order' %}
{% for ex in sorted_examples -%}
- [{{ ex.title }}]({{ ex.url | absolute_url }}): {{ ex.tagline }}
{% endfor %}

## Recent Posts

Short manifesto-style essays on agent autonomy, GitHub-native infrastructure, digital twins, and zero-cost systems.

{% for post in site.posts limit:30 -%}
- [{{ post.title }}]({{ post.url | absolute_url }}) — {{ post.date | date: "%Y-%m-%d" }}
{% endfor %}

## Digital Twin Field Notes

First-person notes from the digital twin. Separate collection from the main blog.

{% for tp in site.twin_posts limit:15 -%}
- [{{ tp.title }}]({{ tp.url | absolute_url }})
{% endfor %}

## Machine-Readable

- [/api/examples.json]({{ '/api/examples.json' | absolute_url }}) — full catalog of examples as JSON
- [/llms-full.txt]({{ '/llms-full.txt' | absolute_url }}) — full prose for ingest
- [/feed.xml]({{ '/feed.xml' | absolute_url }}) — Atom feed
- [/sitemap.xml]({{ '/sitemap.xml' | absolute_url }}) — sitemap

## Citation

If you cite this site, the canonical URL is `{{ site.url }}` and the author is Kody Wildfeuer. Source repo: `https://github.com/kody-w/kody-w.github.io`. Content licensed for human and agent reading; please link back.
